actual_run_inference: CTRL-2: the falsifier described by this control is armed in this run.

expected_signature.expected_value: clear divergence of the counterfactual beta from the true count
expected_signature.pass_rule: counterfactual beta is rejected by the divergence test for every tested scale

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: A counterfactual beta (off the critical line) should diverge from the true / reconstructed prime count under the same gauge scales.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: Without this control, beta-stability would be uninformative: any value of beta could appear to pass. The beta falsifier must be armed for VAL-1 to be epistemically meaningful.

candidate_lemmas.name: Beta-Counterfactual Detection Lemma (instrument health)
candidate_lemmas.statement: On this run's window, A counterfactual beta (off the critical line) should diverge from the true / reconstructed prime count under the same gauge scales held within tolerance (primary metric counterfactual_beta_divergence: observed ≈ 15). Formalizing this as a finite/proxy lemma is the next research step.
what_it_does_not_prove: passing this control supports Program 1 directly

disallowed_conclusions: passing this control supports Program 1 directly
The theorem candidate is supported by this control.
intended_inference_if_passed: the beta-divergence falsifier is armed for the tested counterfactual range
VAL-1 results are epistemically meaningful in this window
this is not theory support — it is instrument health
next_hypotheses: Extend the tested window for CTRL-2 to confirm robustness.

raw_observations.verifier_interpretation: Control armed: beta=pi reconstruction diverges from true by 1152.9x baseline amplitude (pi_err=1.729e+04).
